extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo defended 50-second GC lead in final ITT, losing 41 seconds to stage winner DENNIS Rowan but holding on by 25 seconds overall
tactical_patterns: Dennis gained 41 seconds on Quintana in 10km ITT, revealing ~4sec/km ITT differential between Colombian climbers and TT specialists.
Pinot lost 16-second GC cushion over Dennis despite starting only 11 seconds slower at intermediate, collapsing in final 5.6km.
Quintana preserved GC through climbing dominance on Terminillo despite being vulnerable to sustained attacks stages 5-6 and weak ITT.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: positive: Won 10km ITT as pre-race favorite, gained 1:06 to vault from 3rd to 2nd GC, proving time gaps underestimated his TT superiority
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: Faded badly in final 5.6km of ITT after acceptable start, lost second place to Dennis despite 16-second buffer
rider_signals.VAN EMDEN Jos: positive: Posted provisional best time until Dennis, demonstrating domestique-level ITT strength exceeding climber specialists
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: Lost 41 seconds in 10km ITT but margins sufficient due to Terminillo climbing dominance, vulnerable to longer TTs
